notes.note: S. D. is salutem dicit, "sends greetings". CONLEGAE ("to my colleague") marks Cornificius as Cicero's fellow-member of the college of augurs, the priests who took the auspices; the augurate was a lifelong honour and the shared title carries real warmth.

notes.anchor: From Syria some rather disquieting news
notes.note: The unrest in Syria is the revolt of Q. Caecilius Bassus, who had seized the province from Caesar's governor; it threatened the neighbouring command Cornificius was about to take up.

notes.anchor: On the Best Kind of Oratory
notes.note: Cicero's short treatise De Optimo Genere Oratorum, written as a preface to his (now lost) translations of the rival speeches of Demosthenes and Aeschines on the Crown, arguing for the Attic plain style as the orator's ideal.
